content: Most existing discussions frame Self-Refine as a structural framework for output improvement, while LLM self-reflection mechanisms focus on the perspective design for generating effective self-feedback. A novel hybrid paradigm can be built where multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection research are inserted into each iterative feedback step of the original Self-Refine framework, eliminating its implicit limitation of relying on a single default perspective for self-feedback. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while addressing the common failure mode of single-perspective self-refinement where an LLM repeats its own initial blind spots across iterations.
